nodes.label: ∅ | be | queen
nodes.sentence: Once there was a queen who had a little daughter, too young to walk on her own.

nodes.label: child | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: One day, the child was very difficult to calm, no matter what her mother did.

nodes.label: queen | look | window
nodes.sentence: Growing impatient, the queen looked out the window and saw the ravens flying around the castle.

nodes.label: she | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: She said, “I wish you were a raven and would just fly away so I could have some peace.”

nodes.label: she | speak | child
nodes.sentence: No sooner had she spoken than the child she held was turned into a raven and flew out the window.

nodes.label: bird | fly | forest
nodes.sentence: The bird flew to a dark forest and stayed there a long time, while the parents heard nothing from their daughter.

nodes.label: man | walk | forest
nodes.sentence: Long afterward, a man was walking through the forest when he heard a raven’s call and followed the sound.

nodes.label: raven | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: As he got closer, the raven said, “I was born a king’s daughter, but now I am under a curse.

nodes.label: you | free | i
nodes.sentence: You can free me.”

nodes.label: i | do | what
nodes.sentence: “What must I do?”

nodes.label: man | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: the man asked.

nodes.label: she | tell | he
nodes.sentence: She told him, “Go deeper into the forest until you find a house where an old woman lives.

nodes.label: she | offer | food
nodes.sentence: She will offer you food and drink, but you must not accept either, or you will fall into a deep sleep and won’t be able to help me.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | heap
nodes.sentence: Behind the house is a big tan heap, and you must stand there watching for me.

nodes.label: i | come | day
nodes.sentence: For three days in a row, I will come in my carriage at two o’clock in the afternoon.

nodes.label: it | draw | ∅
nodes.sentence: The first day, it will be drawn by four white horses; the second day by four chestnut horses; and the third by four black horses.

nodes.label: i | free | ∅
nodes.sentence: But if you fall asleep any day, I won’t be freed.”

nodes.label: man | promise | ∅
nodes.sentence: The man promised to do as she said, but the raven sighed, “I fear even now that you will take something from the woman and fail to save me.”

nodes.label: he | promise | ∅
nodes.sentence: He once again promised not to eat or drink anything.

nodes.label: woman | greet | he
nodes.sentence: When the man entered the house, the old woman greeted him, saying, “You look so tired!

nodes.label: ∅ | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: Come and rest; let me give you some food and drink.”

nodes.label: he | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: “No,” he replied, “I won’t eat or drink.”

nodes.label: she | keep | ∅
nodes.sentence: But she kept urging him, “If you won’t eat, at least have a sip of wine; one drink won’t matter.”

nodes.label: he | give | ∅
nodes.sentence: Eventually, he gave in and drank.

nodes.label: he | go | garden
nodes.sentence: As the appointed hour approached, he went out to the garden and climbed the tan heap to watch for the raven.

nodes.label: he | feel | ∅
nodes.sentence: Suddenly, he felt very tired and, despite his determination to stay awake, lay down.

nodes.label: eye | close | moment
nodes.sentence: Within moments his eyes closed and he fell into a deep sleep no noise could break.

nodes.label: she | sigh | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the raven’s carriage arrived, drawn by four white horses, she sighed, “I know he has fallen asleep.”

nodes.label: she | find | ∅
nodes.sentence: She found him sleeping on the tan heap, called to and shook him, but in vain.

nodes.label: woman | offer | food
nodes.sentence: The next day, at noon, the old woman offered him food and drink again, which he again refused at first.

nodes.label: he | take | drink
nodes.sentence: Yet tired by her persistence, he eventually took another drink.

nodes.label: he | go | o’clock
nodes.sentence: At two o’clock, he went to the tan heap to watch for the raven but again felt overwhelmingly tired and fell asleep.

nodes.label: ∅ | find | ∅
nodes.sentence: The raven arrived with her four chestnut horses and sadly admitted, “I know he’s fallen asleep,” then found him asleep and could not wake him.

nodes.label: woman | ask | day
nodes.sentence: On the third day, the old woman asked, “Why won’t you eat or drink?

nodes.label: you | try | ∅
nodes.sentence: Are you trying to harm yourself?”

nodes.label: he | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: He replied firmly that he would neither eat nor drink.

nodes.label: he | smell | wine
nodes.sentence: But when she set down food and wine again, though he tried to resist, he smelled the wine and couldn’t help drinking deeply.

nodes.label: he | go | heap
nodes.sentence: When the time came, he once more went to the tan heap, but he felt even more exhausted than before and fell into an even deeper sleep.

nodes.label: carriage | draw | ∅
nodes.sentence: This time the raven’s carriage was drawn by black horses.

nodes.label: she | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: She was more sorrowful than ever as she said, “I know he has fallen asleep and won’t be able to free me.”

nodes.label: she | find | ∅
nodes.sentence: She found him deeply asleep, and nothing she tried could wake him.

nodes.label: she | place | loaf
nodes.sentence: Then she placed beside him a loaf, some meat, and a flask of wine that never grew any less, no matter how much he ate or drank.

nodes.label: she | take | ring
nodes.sentence: She took a gold ring from her finger, engraved with her name, and put it on his finger.

nodes.label: she | lay | letter
nodes.sentence: Finally, she laid a letter near him, explaining what she had left and writing, “I see you won’t free me as long as you stay here.

nodes.label: it | ’ | power
nodes.sentence: But if you still want to help me, come to the golden castle of Stromberg; it’s well within your power.”

nodes.label: she | return | carriage
nodes.sentence: Then she returned to her carriage and drove to the golden castle of Stromberg.

nodes.label: he | feel | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the man woke and realized how long he'd slept, he felt sad.

nodes.label: she | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: “She must have been here and left.

nodes.label: it | ’ | ∅
nodes.sentence: It’s too late now to save her.”

nodes.label: eye | land | loaf
nodes.sentence: His eyes landed on the loaf, meat, wine, ring, and letter.

nodes.label: he | understand | everything
nodes.sentence: Reading the letter, he understood everything.

nodes.label: he | get | ∅
nodes.sentence: He got up immediately, eager to set out for Stromberg, though he didn’t know which way to go.

nodes.label: he | travel | time
nodes.sentence: He traveled for a long time, finally reaching a dark forest.

nodes.label: he | walk | it
nodes.sentence: He walked through it for fourteen days but couldn’t find a way out.

nodes.label: he | sleep | bush
nodes.sentence: One night, exhausted, he slept under a bush.

nodes.label: he | hear | ∅
nodes.sentence: The next day, while looking for a place to rest, he heard howling and crying that kept him awake.

nodes.label: he | see | light
nodes.sentence: When it grew darker and people began lighting their homes, he saw a faint light ahead and went toward it.

nodes.label: he | find | house
nodes.sentence: He found a house that looked smaller than it really was compared to a huge giant standing in front of it.

nodes.label: he | think | ∅
nodes.sentence: He thought, “If the giant sees me entering, my life might be at risk.”

nodes.label: he | gather | courage
nodes.sentence: Still, he gathered courage and went on.

nodes.label: giant | shout | ∅
nodes.sentence: The giant shouted, “Lucky you came!

nodes.label: i | starve | ∅
nodes.sentence: I’m starving and could make you my supper.”

nodes.label: man | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “I’d rather you didn’t,” said the man.

nodes.label: i | give | myself
nodes.sentence: “I won’t just give myself up.

nodes.label: i | have | food
nodes.sentence: But if you’re hungry, I have enough food and drink to satisfy you.”

nodes.label: i | leave | you
nodes.sentence: “If that’s true,” the giant said, “I’ll leave you alone.

nodes.label: i | think | ∅
nodes.sentence:
I only thought of eating you because I was so hungry.”

Inside, the man brought out the endless bread, meat, and wine.

nodes.label: giant | eat | ∅
nodes.sentence: The giant ate and drank heartily and was very pleased.

nodes.label: man | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: Afterward, the man asked if the giant could show him the castle of Stromberg.

nodes.label: giant | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: The giant said he’d check his map, which showed all towns and villages.

nodes.label: he | find | castle
nodes.sentence: But he couldn’t find the castle.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “No problem,” he said.

nodes.label: i | have | map
nodes.sentence: “I have larger maps upstairs.”

nodes.label: they | search | those
nodes.sentence: They searched those but still found no sign of it.

nodes.label: man | want | ∅
nodes.sentence: The man wanted to continue his journey, but the giant asked him to stay a day or two until his brother returned from gathering food.

nodes.label: they | ask | he
nodes.sentence: When the brother came back, they asked him about the castle.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: He said he’d look after eating.

nodes.label: they | go | room
nodes.sentence: Once fed, they all went to his room to search through more maps.

nodes.label: they | find | castle
nodes.sentence: After some time, they found the castle, but it was thousands of miles away.

nodes.label: i | reach | it
nodes.sentence: “How will I reach it?”

nodes.label: man | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: the man asked.

nodes.label: giant | offer | ∅
nodes.sentence: “I’ve two hours free,” the giant offered.

nodes.label: i | return | ∅
nodes.sentence: “I’ll carry you close to the castle, then I must return to care for the child we’re watching.”

nodes.label: giant | carry | man
nodes.sentence: The giant carried the man to within about a hundred leagues of Stromberg castle, then left him, saying, “You can walk the rest yourself.”

nodes.label: man | travel | ∅
nodes.sentence: The man traveled day and night until he reached the golden castle, which sat atop a glass mountain.

nodes.label: he | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: Looking up, he saw the cursed maiden driving around her castle before going inside.

nodes.label: attempt | make | ∅
nodes.sentence: He was overjoyed but couldn’t climb the slippery mountain; every attempt made him fall back.

nodes.label: he | decide | ∅
nodes.sentence: Saddened, he decided to wait for her and built a small hut to live in, watching her daily for a whole year.

nodes.label: he | see | robber
nodes.sentence: One day, from his hut, he saw three robbers fighting and called out, “God be with you.”

nodes.label: they | stop | ∅
nodes.sentence: They stopped temporarily, looked around, then returned to fighting more fiercely.

nodes.label: he | call | more
nodes.sentence: He called again twice more, and the third time, curious, he went out and asked why they were fighting.

nodes.label: one | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: One said he had found a stick that could open any door by striking it.

nodes.label: another | have | cloak
nodes.sentence: Another had a cloak making its wearer invisible.

nodes.label: third | catch | horse
nodes.sentence: The third caught a horse that could overcome any obstacle, even climb glass mountains.

nodes.label: they | agree | ∅
nodes.sentence: They couldn’t agree whether to keep these treasures together or split up.

nodes.label: man | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: The man said, “I’ll trade you something far more valuable—not money, since I have none, but something better.

nodes.label: i | test | ∅
nodes.sentence: First, I’ll test if your things really work.”

nodes.label: robber | give | horse
nodes.sentence: The robbers gave him the horse, stick, and cloak.

nodes.label: he | vanish | sight
nodes.sentence: Putting on the cloak, he vanished from sight.

nodes.label: he | strike | they
nodes.sentence: Then he struck at them with the stick, beating them one by one, shouting, “There, you lazy scoundrels, now are you satisfied?”

nodes.label: he | mount | horse
nodes.sentence: He mounted the horse and rode up the glass mountain.

nodes.label: he | find | gate
nodes.sentence: At the castle gate, he found it closed but struck it with the stick, and the door flew open.

nodes.label: he | climb | step
nodes.sentence: He climbed the steps and entered the room where the maiden sat with a golden goblet of wine.

nodes.label: he | throw | ring
nodes.sentence: Still invisible under the cloak, he threw the ring she had given him into her cup.

nodes.label: that | ’ | ring
nodes.sentence: “That’s my own ring!”

nodes.label: she | exclaim | ∅
nodes.sentence: she exclaimed.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: “If that’s true, then the man destined to free me must be here.”

nodes.label: she | search | ∅
nodes.sentence: She searched everywhere but couldn’t find him.

nodes.label: he | go | ∅
nodes.sentence: Meanwhile, he had gone outside, mounted the horse, and removed his cloak.

nodes.label: she | see | he
nodes.sentence: When she reached the castle gate, she saw him and cried for joy.

nodes.label: he | get | horse
nodes.sentence: He got off the horse, took her in his arms, and she kissed him.

nodes.label: she | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Now you have truly freed me,” she said.

nodes.label: we | celebrate | wedding
nodes.sentence: “Tomorrow we will celebrate our wedding.”
